extracted_information: The article demonstrates how to achieve real-time collaborative editing in the browser using Yjs, a CRDT (Conflict-free Replicated Data Type) library, combined with WebSockets for syncing shared state across users. Yjs handles conflict resolution and offline editing, offering efficient synchronization. Unlike Operational Transformation (OT), Yjs's CRDT approach ensures natural convergence without requiring a central server for conflict resolution. The setup involves creating a WebSocket server using `express` and `ws`, and a frontend HTML page that connects to the WebSocket server using `y-websocket` and binds a `Y.Text` type to a textarea for real-time updates.

specifications.conflict_resolution_mechanism: CRDTs, which naturally converge without a central server for conflict resolution (unlike OT algorithms)

features: Scalability for various collaborative tools (e.g., shared editors, whiteboards, note-taking apps)
Supports integration with rich text editors (e.g., CodeMirror, Monaco Editor)
Supports user awareness features (e.g., cursors, names, colors) via `y-protocols/awareness`
Supports state persistence with databases (e.g., LevelDB, MongoDB)
Supports integration of authentication for permissioned access
